summary.intent: Add informational metadata to the session compaction notification message, telling users which compaction mode was used, token usage for LLM-based modes, and how to change the setting.
summary.outcome: Introduced a `CompactionOutcome` struct carrying mode + optional token usage, threaded it through all four strategy implementations and the dispatcher, and surfaced it in both the `auto_compact` broadcast event and the `ChatService::compact` RPC response. Two new tests added; all 221 chat + 185 config tests pass with clippy and fmt clean in both feature configurations.

repo: The compaction pipeline lives in `crates/chat/src/compaction_run/` and is dispatched from `mod.rs`; callers in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` consume only the public surface (`run_compaction`, `extract_summary_body`, `CompactionRunError`).
Feature-gated modules (`llm-compaction`) are gated at the `mod` declaration level in `mod.rs`, so individual files inside those modules need no internal `#[cfg]` guards.

Broadcast events for compaction results are emitted in two places in `lib.rs`: the standalone `compact_session` path and the `run_with_tools` auto-compact path; both needed updating to carry the new metadata.
The project uses a fixed nightly toolchain (`nightly-2025-11-30`) for fmt, clippy, and tests.
Cross-module visibility within `compaction_run/**` is scoped to `pub(super)`; nothing leaks to the wider crate.

code.finding: Dispatcher now returns `CompactionOutcome` (history + mode enum + optional token count) instead of raw history; also exposes `SETTINGS_HINT` string for the user-facing message.
Strategy `run()` updated to return `CompactionOutcome` with `mode: CompactionMode::Deterministic` and `tokens_used: None`.
Strategy `run()` returns `CompactionOutcome`; tests updated to unwrap `.history` from the outcome.
Strategy `run()` returns `CompactionOutcome` with LLM token count populated; tests updated accordingly.
Strategy `run()` returns `CompactionOutcome` with LLM token count populated; tests updated accordingly.
Both compaction call sites updated: `compact_session` standalone and `run_with_tools` auto-compact broadcast now include mode and token metadata from `CompactionOutcome`.

workflow: Iterative edit-compile-fix cycle: after threading the new return type the code failed to compile; fixes were applied incrementally before running the full test/fmt/clippy suite.
fmt issues surfaced separately from clippy and required targeted diff inspection to locate the offending formatting in broadcast payload rewrites.
Running `cargo test --lib` on both `moltis-chat` and `moltis-config` crates independently gives fast confidence before the final push.
Validating both `--all-features` and `--no-default-features` clippy runs is part of the standard pre-commit gate for this repo.

friction: Compile error after threading `CompactionOutcome` through all strategies required a fix pass before tests could run.
A rustfmt issue in the broadcast payload rewrite wasn't caught until the dedicated `fmt` check step, requiring additional targeted edits.
open_items: Greptile re-indexing of the new module structure was noted as pending after the refactor commit (`7c0eef4c`) â€” its results were not yet visible at session end.

The `send()` â†’ `self.compact()` auto-compact wrapper at the call site was left lean (not surfacing full metadata directly) with a note that the inner `chat.compact done` broadcast covers it â€” may be worth revisiting for consistency.
No frontend/UI changes were made to render the new broadcast metadata fields; consumer-side display of mode name, token count, and settings hint is presumably deferred to a follow-up.
